title: Task 3: Synthetic-check hook for rotation window
design: Hook a synthetic check that runs every 30s during rotation window; assert zero 5xx. Files: src/lib/vault_rotate.py, tests/integration/test_rotate_synthetic.py.

title: Task 2: Add vault_rotate orchestration module
design: Create src/lib/vault_rotate.py with rotate(name, new_value) that calls vault.rotate_secret + schedules T+5min expiry. Files: src/lib/vault_rotate.py, tests/lib/test_vault_rotate.py.

title: Task 1: Add rotate_secret helper to vault.py
notes:
Implementation complete. Awaiting audit.

Files changed:
- src/lib/vault.py: added rotate_secret(name, new_value) + per-name locking + module-level _aliases store. _write_alias placeholder is monkeypatchable for unreachable simulation.
- tests/lib/test_vault.py: 8 new tests (spec required 5; added empty-name + parallel-different-names for coverage).

Test results: 9/9 pass (pytest tests/).

Spec deviations:
- Spec mentions 'acme.errors.VaultError' but no acme.errors module exists in the repo; VaultError already lived in vault.py so I kept it there. Suggest spec-refinement clarify or create acme.errors.
- Empty/None new_value raises ValueError (not VaultError) — input-validation is a caller bug, not a vault failure. Re-classify if spec intends VaultError.

Edge cases covered: same-name serialization, different-name parallelism, unreachable backend (no partial write), empty/None value rejection, empty name rejection.

title: Epic: rotate service credentials without downtime [sp001]
